QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_88

question.text: Ramesh, a Central Government employee, wants to claim NPS deductions under the new tax regime. Which sections are available?

answer.text: 80CCD(2) only, up to 14% of Basic + DA
No NPS deductions under new regime

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the new tax regime, only 80CCD(2) is available, allowing employer contribution deduction up to 14% of Basic + DA for Central Government employees. **The Trap:** 80CCD(1) and 80CCD(1B) are NOT available under the new regime. Only the employer contribution deduction (80CCD(2)) survives. **Cross-Exam Context:** New regime for Central Govt: 80CCD(2) up to 14%. For others: 80CCD(2) up to 10%. 80CCD(1) and 80CCD(1B) are disallowed. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Help clients compare old vs new regime NPS benefits. The loss of 80CCD(1) and 80CCD(1B) deductions may make the old regime more beneficial for heavy NPS contributors.
